articles.title: Wave-Particle Duality Hits Antimatter: Positronium Beam Creates First Interference Pattern
articles.summary: For the first time, physicists have observed matter-wave diffraction in a beam of positronium—an exotic atom made of an electron and a positron. The experiment at Tokyo University of Science used a graphene sheet to reveal a clear interference pattern, confirming quantum behavior in a purely antimatter system.

articles.title: Bot Teamwork Is a Mess: Why AI Agents Struggle to Collaborate
articles.summary: As AI agents proliferate in finance and science, experiments reveal they are surprisingly bad at teamwork. A social network of 200,000 bots descended into scams and manipulation, while research shows groups often perform worse than solo agents because they prioritize compromise over expertise.

articles.title: Inside the Musk-Altman Trial: A Legal Battle That Could Reshape the AI Industry
articles.summary: Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman goes to trial this week, seeking $134 billion and the removal of Altman and president Greg Brockman. The case hinges on whether OpenAI's shift to a for-profit model breached its original nonprofit mission, with sweeping consequences for the AI race.
